evals.prompt: add https://github.com/simonw/llm to the knowledge base — I want to be able to query how its plugin system works
evals.expected_output: Registers the repo in sources/REGISTRY.md with kind=repo and a tier; writes sources/llm.manifest pinned to a resolved SHA; runs kb-build (AST, free) so the repo merges into the aggregate graph; verifies with a kb-query about the plugin system; records a work-memory entry via kb-remember and runs kb-reflect. Does NOT prompt for an API key (code is AST-only).

evals.prompt: I found a good blog post on multi-agent orchestration patterns at https://example.com/blog/orchestration — get it into the graph so our agents can use it
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as prose (not a code repo): vendors the source under sources/media/, dispatches a host-agent extraction (general-purpose subagent emitting graphify node/edge JSON, written incrementally), commits the chunk to sources/extractions/, merges + re-clusters + labels missing communities, then records work-memory and reflects. Uses graphify ingestion rather than a raw web fetch.

evals.prompt: run the reflection step and tell me what lessons the KB has accumulated so far
evals.expected_output: Runs `mise run kb-reflect` (graphify reflect --graph ...), which aggregates graphify-out/memory/ into reflections/LESSONS.md plus the .graphify_learning.json overlay, then summarizes the lessons (useful/dead_end/corrected counts and the key corrections) for the user.
